dialogueChunks.intro: Please, {player_name}, you have to help us. Our stores are nearly empty and the children haven't had a proper meal in days.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: We need {quest_item} desperately. I've heard there might be some {location_hint}, but none of us are brave enough to make the journey. The {enemy_type} have been prowling the roads and we've already lost two foragers this season.
dialogueChunks.outro: You'd be saving lives, truly. I wouldn't ask if there were any other way.

playerResponses.text: I'll find what you need. Stay safe until I return.
Fine, but I expect fair compensation for the danger.
I'm sorry, I can't help right now.

dialogueChunks.intro: Oh thank the stars, a traveler! {player_name}, I beg you, hear me out before you move on.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A sickness has taken hold in {location}. {target_npc} fell ill first, and now half the settlement can barely stand. The alchemist says we need {quest_item} for a remedy, but it only grows in dangerous territory {location_hint}. I'd go myself but my hands won't stop shaking.
dialogueChunks.outro: I know it's a lot to ask of a stranger. But strangers are all we have left to turn to.

playerResponses.text: Tell me exactly where to look. I'll bring it back.
I'll do it, but only because the children are suffering.
That sounds like a problem for a healer, not me.

dialogueChunks.intro: Wait, please don't walk away! I know how this looks, a grown person begging, but I have nowhere else to turn, {player_name}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Raiders came through {location} two nights ago. Took everything: our {quest_item}, our tools, anything they could carry. {target_npc} tried to stop them but they were outnumbered. We tracked them heading {location_hint}, but none of us can fight like you can.
dialogueChunks.outro: If we don't get our supplies back before the cold sets in, this settlement won't survive the season. Please.

playerResponses.text: Point me toward their trail. I'll get everything back.
I suppose I could track them down, for the right price.
Chasing raiders isn't something I do for free.

dialogueChunks.intro: Forgive me for grabbing your arm like that, {player_name}. I wouldn't do it if I weren't out of options.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A flood tore through {location} three days ago. Swept away the bridge, the mill, and half our winter stores. {target_npc} managed to save the youngest children but got hurt doing it and can barely walk now. We've scraped together what little we have left, but without {quest_item} we can't rebuild the mill or grind grain before the frost comes. Last I heard, there's a supply {location_hint}, but the water drove the {enemy_type} out of the lowlands and they're roaming everywhere.
dialogueChunks.outro: I know you owe us nothing. But without that mill, we starve before spring.

playerResponses.text: I'll get what you need. Focus on keeping everyone warm.
Flood damage is rough. I'll help, but I won't do it for free.
There's nothing I can do about a flood. I'm sorry.

dialogueChunks.intro: You don't know me, {player_name}, and I have no right to burden you with our troubles. But my people are dying.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: We're refugees from a settlement that was overrun by {enemy_type} a week ago. Barely made it to {location} with the clothes on our backs. The folk here have been kind, but there isn't enough food or medicine for all of us. {target_npc} sent word that there's {quest_item} stored in our old home {location_hint}, abandoned when we fled. Nobody here will go back: they're too afraid of what's still lurking there.
dialogueChunks.outro: Everything we need to survive is sitting in a place we're too broken to return to. If you could bring it back, you'd be saving entire families.

playerResponses.text: Tell me the way. I'll bring back everything I can carry.
Heading into overrun territory is serious. What can you offer?
I can't fight an entire warband alone. You need soldiers.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I've asked every merchant, every traveler, every guard that's passed through. You're the last chance I have.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The well in {location} went dry two weeks ago. We've been rationing what little water we had stored, but it ran out yesterday. {target_npc} found an old map showing an underground spring {location_hint}, but the tunnels down there are crawling with {enemy_type}. We sent a small group to try and reach it, and only one came back. She said the {quest_item} we need to repair the well pump is down there too, just sitting in the ruins.
dialogueChunks.outro: Children are drinking muddy rainwater from barrels, {player_name}. I'll get on my knees if that's what it takes.

playerResponses.text: Stay on your feet. I'll handle the tunnels.
I'll go, but you owe me when this is over.
If your own people couldn't survive down there, neither can I.

dialogueChunks.intro: I can see you're busy, {player_name}. I wouldn't stop you if lives weren't at stake.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Our blacksmith collapsed at the forge yesterday. Heatstroke, the healer says, but really it's exhaustion: she's been working day and night trying to repair our tools after the {enemy_type} wrecked the workshop. Without proper tools, we can't harvest, can't hunt, can't defend ourselves. {target_npc} says if we can get {quest_item} from the old mining camp {location_hint}, the blacksmith can get us back on our feet. But that camp has been abandoned for good reason.
dialogueChunks.outro: We're not warriors or adventurers. We're farmers with broken plows and empty hands. Please.

playerResponses.text: Point me to the camp. I'll bring back what she needs.
Broken tools and monsters? You really are in trouble. Alright, I'll go.
I'm no errand runner. Find another way.

dialogueChunks.intro: Traveler! Wait, please. I saw you walking past and I couldn't let you go without asking.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} went out three days ago to deliver {quest_item} to a neighboring settlement and never arrived. The road {location_hint} has been dangerous lately: {enemy_type} sightings every night, and tracks all along the ridge. We found the cart abandoned halfway, wheel broken, supplies scattered. No blood, thank the stars, but no sign of {target_npc} either. Someone took them, or something chased them off the road.
dialogueChunks.outro: That delivery was everything we had to trade for seed grain. Without it, and without {target_npc}, we won't make it to harvest.

playerResponses.text: I'll find {target_npc} and recover the supplies. You have my word.
Missing person and stolen goods? That's two jobs. I expect to be paid for both.
Search parties are not my specialty. I hope they turn up.
